# daytona_api_key (Resource)

Manages a Daytona API key.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "daytona_api_key" "example" {
  name = "terraform-example"

  permissions = [
    "write:sandboxes",
    "delete:sandboxes",
    "read:volumes",
  ]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) API key name.
- `permissions` (Set of String) Daytona organization resource permissions assigned to the API key.

### Optional

- `expires_at` (String) Optional RFC3339 timestamp when the API key expires.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) API key creation timestamp.
- `id` (String) API key name, used as the resource ID.
- `last_used_at` (String) API key last-used timestamp, when available.
- `user_id` (String) ID of the user who owns the API key.
- `value` (String, Sensitive) API key value. Daytona returns this only when the key is created.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import daytona_api_key.example "terraform-example"
```
